title: Mahua seed Grading and Marking Rules 1982
description: Mahua seed Grading and Marking Rules 1982
text: MAHUA SEED GRADING AND MARKING RULES Short title, application and commencement :- (i) These rules may be called the Mahua Seed Grading and Marking Rules, 1982. (ii) They shall apply to the Mahua Seed produced in India. (iii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. Definitions:— In these rules, otherwise requires;~ unless the context (i) "Agricultural Marketing Adviser” means the Agricultural Marketing Adviser to the Government of India; (ii) "Schedule" means a schedule appended to these rules; (iii) "Authorised packer" means a person or a body ofpersons who has been granted a Certificate of Authorisation by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser for getting the commodity graded and agmarked in accordance with the grade standards and procedure prescribed under the rules; , (iv) "Certificate" means Certificate of Authorisation. Grade Designation.:- The grade designations to indicate the quality ofMahua Seed shall be as set out in columns I of Schedule I. Definitions of quality.:- The quality indicated by the grade designations shall be as set out against each grade designation in columns 2 to 7 of Schedule. Grade designation mark.:- The grade designation mark shall consist of a label specifying the grade designation and bearing a design consisting of outline map of India with the word" AGMARK" and figure of the rising sun with the words "Produce of India and "th21 W" resembling the mark as set out in Schedule II. 50 Method of Marking .:- (l) The grade designation mark shall be securely affixed to each package in a manner approved by the Agricultural marketing Adviser. (2) In addition to the grade designation, the following particulars shall also be clearly marked on the label 1 (a) Date of Packing : (b) Lot Number : (3) Each container shall have indelibly marked on it the following details : (a) Lot Number; (b) Name and address ofpacker}. (c) Net weight; and (d) Any other particulars as may be specified by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser from time to time. (4) The authorised packer may, alter obtaining the prior approval of the Agricultural Marketing Adviser, mark his private trade mark on a container in manner approved by the said officer, provided that the private trade mark does not represent a quality or grade of Mahua seed different train that indicated by the grade designation mark affixed to the container in accordance with these rules. Method of packing.:- (l)Mahua seed shall be packed in sound jute bag or any other type of container and of capacity and in such manner as may be specified fi-om time to time by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser. (2) Packing material shall be clean and dry, free fiom fungus and insect attack and obnoxious smell. (3) Each package shall contain Mahua seed of one grade designation only. (4) Each package shall be securely closed an sealed in the manner proscribed by the Agricultural marketing Adviser. -- 1 of 3 -- SCHEDULE I (See rules 3 and 4) Grade designations and definitions of quality of Malina seed produced in Endia Grade Definition of quality designation 7_ ‘_#7 m _ "WWW“ 77v ”7 M“ .,__,.., _ Special characteristics _ General Characteristics Colour Foreign Damaged, Broken Moisture, matter, slightly and per cent percent damaged fragments by by weight and per cent weight (Maximum) weevilled by (Maxim um) seeds, per weight cent (Maximum) by weight (Maximum) 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 I Redish yellow or 2.0 4.6 2.0 6.0 Mahua seed shall be : yellowish brown or (1) the cotyledons light brown obtained after decorticating the seeds fiom the fruit borne on the tree Madhuca indica J.F. H Dark Brown 4.0 10.0 6.0 6.0 Gmolin, syn, Madhuca latifolia (Roxb) III Dark Brown 6.0 15.0 10.0 6.0 Macbride or Madhuca longifolia (Roofing) Macbride, beloinging to the family Sapotaceae. (2) Wholesome, free from moulds, weevils, obnoxious smell, deleterious substances and all other impurities except to the extent indicated in the schedule. (3) have uniform shape, size and colour. Definitions : Foreign m atter Damaged seeds Slighty damaged seeds Weevilled seeds , Brokens Fragments shall include dust stones, lumps of earth or leaves, any other edible or non—edible seeds shall be the seeds which are damaged mechanically or by mould or those showing internal discolouration or seeds materially affecting the quality shall be the seeds that are superficially damaged or discoloured, damaged and discolouration not materially affecting the quality shall be those seeds which are partially or wholly bored or eaten by weevils or other insects. shall include those seeds which are less than three-fourth but more than one-fourth of a whole seed. shall include those seeds which are less than one-fourth ofa whole seed. 51 -- 2 of 3 -- SCHEDULE-II (See rule 5) Grade designation mark (Published inthe Gazette ofIndia, Part-II, Section 3 (ii) under 8.0. 2648 Dated 24-7-1982) 52 -- 3 of 3 --
